sourceMovie.title: Resident Evil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Resident Evil works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Resident Evil excels in horror, Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Resident Evil: Extinction (2007) is the natural next step after Resident Evil: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Resident Evil who respond to its horror will find Extinction (2015) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Logan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Resident Evil fans keep landing on Logan (2017) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Men in Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Men in Black draws from the same action well as Resident Evil, channeling science-fiction in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1997 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Divergent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Divergent (2014) captures the action appeal of Resident Evil without simply imitating it. The science-fiction streak running through both films is what puts Divergent at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revolutions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Resident Evil — that tense edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into The Matrix Revolutions (2003). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Dune
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Dune (1984) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Resident Evil rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Running Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Running Man (1987) is the kind of film Resident Evil fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Videodrome
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Videodrome shares Resident Evil's gift for horror — the 1983 film delivers science-fiction without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Resident Evil?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Resident Evil: Extinction, Extinction—each shares DNA with Resident Evil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Resident Evil?
faq.answer: Resident Evil (2002) is best described as Horror, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Resident Evil part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Resident Evil Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
